name: stack-container
role: Vertical flex column with a single gap between children. The foundational primitive for any vertical content rhythm — composes into prose body sections, sidebar lists, form-field groups, card stacks. Carries no aesthetic concern of its own; gap + alignment come from tokens.

gap.note: xs=--space-1 (4px), sm=--space-2 (8px), md=--space-3 (12px), lg=--space-card (24px), xl=--space-section (64px). Tokens come from tokens-baseline.

align.note: Cross-axis (horizontal) alignment of children via align-items.

recursive.note: When true, recursively applies gap to all descendant elements via the lobotomized-owl selector (:where(*) > * + *). Useful when the children themselves contain stacks and you want one declaration to govern the entire subtree.

semanticStructure: <!-- any flow content; children may themselves be stacks -->

ariaPatterns.noLandmark: stack-container is purely structural. It carries no role, no aria-label. Operators wrap a stack in a semantic landmark (<section>, <article>, <aside>) when one is needed; the stack itself is the layout, not the semantics.

compositionExamples: Inside prim-center: <prim-center><prim-stack>...</prim-stack></prim-center> — centered max-width column with internal vertical rhythm.
Inside prim-sidebar's sidebar-aside slot: <prim-sidebar-aside><prim-stack data-gap='sm'>...</prim-stack></prim-sidebar-aside> — sidebar nav with consistent spacing between items.

Inside prim-grid cells:
...
 — each grid cell is its own internal stack.

name: stack-separator
role: Optional visual divider between stack children. Renders as a horizontal rule with token-driven color + thickness. Use when the stack's children represent semantically distinct chunks that benefit from a visible break (e.g., between settings sections, between author bio and post list).

ariaPatterns.presentationalHr: <hr> already conveys separator semantics; no role override needed. AT users hear 'separator' or get a contextual pause depending on screen reader.
